title: lathyrus pannonicus subsp. austriacus
intro: Lathyrus pannonicus subsp. austriacus, also known as the Austrian pea or wild pea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain a compound called lathyrine with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of consuming Lathyrus pannonicus subsp. austriacus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as a natural antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of Lathyrus pannonicus subsp. austriacus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
